mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand as discount rates compress and real yields turn negative, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.05% as of 2020-05-01 reflects emergency zero-bound policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window during zero-bound periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month employment growth (PAYEMS) decelerates sharply, signaling weakening labor demand and imminent earnings pressure. This triggers equity repricing downward over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below -500 thousand persons
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132,912 thousand as of 2020-05-01, reflecting COVID-19 employment shock
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move in tandem, jointly signaling consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, aggregate demand expectations rise, supporting positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 403,946 million and HOUST at 891 thousand as of 2020-04-01
failure_condition: Joint positive RSAFS and HOUST signals produce positive 20-day SPX returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month, indicating supply-side weakness and potential stagflationary pressure. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins compress and growth expectations fall.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.5919 (vintage) / 84.5619 (revised) as of 2020-04-01, reflecting pandemic production collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued (year-over-year growth < 2.5%) while FEDFUNDS is at zero, indicating the Fed has room to maintain accommodation without inflation risk. This regime supports equity valuations and produces positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 255.768 and FEDFUNDS at 0.05% as of 2020-05-01
failure_condition: Joint signal (low inflation + zero rates) produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or the interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises sharply month-over-month by more than 0.5 percentage points, signaling acute labor market deterioration. This triggers immediate equity repricing downward as earnings forecasts are cut and recession risk rises, with effect visible over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 13.3% (vintage) / 13.2% (revised) as of 2020-05-01, reflecting pandemic unemployment spike
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and 5-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter shows contraction (negative growth rate). This signals recession and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as earnings expectations collapse and equity risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,974.702 billion (vintage) / 20,709.212 billion (revised) as of 2020-01-01, with large revision indicating data uncertainty during pandemic
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 54% of instances when GDPC1 contracts, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and employment (PAYEMS) both decline simultaneously, indicating broad-based supply and labor contraction. This joint deterioration signals stagflationary pressure and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -1% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < -100 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised down to 84.5619 and PAYEMS at 132,912 thousand as of 2020-05-01
regime_hint: supply-side shock with labor market weakness
failure_condition: Joint signal (production + employment decline) produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 56% of instances, or the interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 18-month window.
